license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.q92dz8e
title: DNA Separation and Detection on Agarose Gels/Post-Electrophoresis Staining Protocol
author: LI-COR Biosciences

steps_raw.instruction: Prepare desired agarose (0.8%, 1.0%, 1.2%, etc.) in 1X TAE or 1X TBE buffer.
Cool solution until warm to the touch (60°F) prior to pouring in casting tray.
Pour molten agarose solution into casting tray and set comb into place. Allow gel to solidify.
Remove comb and place gel in buffer tank.
Prepare enough buffer (1X TAE or 1X TBE) to fill the apparatus.
Prepare samples with loading buffer and load in gel.

Prepare enough solution of 1X TAE or water to cover the agarose gel. • Ethidium Bromide — Add 5 µL of 10 mg/mL EtBr stock solution to 100 mL of buffer (final concentration: 0.5 µg/mL). • SYBR Safe — Add 10 µL of 10,000X SYBR Safe stock solution to 100 mL of buffer (final concentration:1X).
Soak gel for 20 minutes in the prepared solution. 00:20:00, Soak
Destain in water for 20 minutes. Repeat as necessary to remove background from the gel. 00:20:00, Destain
Image on the Odyssey Fc Imager using the 600 channel. Refer to the Image Acquisition on the Odyssey Fc Imager protocol for more information.
